pattern: A LIFO stack can be simulated on FIFO queues by rotating the queue after each push so the newest element sits at the front, making pop and top read the queue's head.
hints: A queue only lets you remove from the front, but a stack must remove the most recently added element, so the data structure has to keep the newest element reachable first.
After enqueuing a new value, rotate every earlier element from front to back so the just-added value ends up at the front of the queue.
With that rotation on push, the queue's front is always the stack top: pop dequeues it, top peeks it, and empty checks the size.

edge_inputs.description: push two then top, pop, and check emptiness
single push leaves a non-empty stack whose top and pop agree
interleaved pushes and pops must preserve LIFO order across refills
popping back to empty then pushing again starts fresh

stress.description: long randomized push/pop/top/empty sequence, querying only when non-empty
stress.note: top/pop only issued when the stack is non-empty; at most ~90 ops per constraints
